rows.backup_posture: Tier 0 direct backup; exact replay cannot reconstruct these from projections.
rows.eventing_posture: Changes append outbox records and append-only outcome references; never inferred from broker history.
rows.label: Manifest and execution-basis artifacts
rows.rationale: These artifacts are the canonical execution and lineage spine and therefore remain transactional source-of-truth rather than append-only viewer cache or stream payload.
rows.retention_posture: Basis-preserving replay retention applies through the full lawful review window.

rows.backup_posture: Tier 0 direct backup; release and restore governance cannot be reconstructed from CI dashboards or deploy logs.
rows.eventing_posture: Promotion, rollback, and restore gates may publish events, but governance truth remains in persisted control objects.
rows.label: Release, migration, and recovery control objects
rows.rationale: These records freeze schema-reader windows, restore evidence, and rollback-vs-fail-forward posture, so they must stay in the same durable control plane as other legal truth.
rows.retention_posture: Governed by release/resilience proof retention and historical reader-window protection.

rows.backup_posture: Tier 0 direct backup for authority-facing settlement and workflow control.
rows.eventing_posture: Mutations emit durable receipts, outbox work, audit events, and projection refreshes.
rows.label: Workflow, filing, and submission state
rows.rationale: The corpus explicitly forbids inferring settled legal posture from UI or transport, so these mutable entities belong in the transactional system of record.
rows.retention_posture: Retention-controlled mutable state with linked append-only evidence refs.

rows.backup_posture: Tier 0 backup because queue rebuild and client recovery anchor on these records.
rows.eventing_posture: Queue drains from these ledgers; queue loss is recoverable from them.
rows.label: Durable command receipts and outbox/inbox ledgers
rows.rationale: The northbound and resilience contracts require durable receipts and outbox/inbox truth before transport or reconnect can be trusted.
rows.retention_posture: Receipts may age out of fast paths, but durable recovery anchors remain queryable for their governed window.

rows.backup_posture: Tier 0 direct backup because legal resend and reconciliation posture depend on these records.
rows.eventing_posture: Queue jobs and callbacks update from these records; they are never reconstructed from broker timing alone.
rows.label: Authority interaction control records
rows.rationale: Authority recovery law requires resend and reconciliation control to survive queue loss and restore independently of transport artifacts.
rows.retention_posture: Regulated control objects with durable lineage to request/response evidence.

rows.backup_posture: Tier 0 direct backup when retention posture gates reopen or legal visibility.
rows.eventing_posture: Retention transitions emit append-only events and proofs, but current lifecycle state stays mutable here.
rows.label: Retention and privacy control objects
rows.rationale: Retention tags, artifact-retention state, and privacy-reconciliation outcomes are mutable governance truth, not derived views.
rows.retention_posture: This is the retention-control layer itself.

rows.backup_posture: Tier 0 direct backup; request and response bodies plus identity hashes are replay and forensics evidence.
rows.eventing_posture: Workers may dispatch or normalize from them, but the envelopes themselves remain immutable append-only artifacts.
rows.label: Authority request and response envelope evidence
rows.rationale: Sealed authority envelopes are evidence-bearing append-only artifacts linked from mutable interaction control records rather than mutable state in their own right.
rows.retention_posture: Regulated records with explicit body refs and lineage preservation.

rows.backup_posture: Tier 0 direct backup because restore and no-blind-resend posture depend on first-seen ingress checkpoints.
rows.eventing_posture: Callbacks, polls, and recovery reads may create more receipts, but canonical receipts stay immutable.
rows.label: Authority ingress receipts and duplicate-suppression checkpoints
rows.rationale: The ingress protocol treats receipts as durable checkpoints that must exist before legal-state mutation or duplicate suppression.
rows.retention_posture: Regulated ingress evidence with explicit dedupe and quarantine posture.

rows.backup_posture: Tier 0 direct backup; audit continuity is a reopen gate.
rows.eventing_posture: Append-only writes only; projections or queries read from this ledger.
rows.label: Audit event ledger
rows.rationale: Audit evidence is explicitly distinct from mutable operational state and must remain append-only under rebuild, rollback, and retention pressure.
rows.retention_posture: Payloads may limit or tombstone, but object, reason, and lineage minimums must survive.

rows.backup_posture: Tier 0 direct backup for decisive-path replay and explainability.
rows.eventing_posture: Graph evidence appends alongside audit events; read-side graph views are disposable.
rows.label: Provenance graph evidence
rows.rationale: The provenance contract requires causally ordered, partitioned, append-only evidence paths rather than ephemeral graph cache reconstruction.
rows.retention_posture: Retention-limited placeholders remain explicit instead of removing path segments.

rows.backup_posture: Tier 0 direct backup because audit claims and DR claims rely on bound historical evidence.
rows.eventing_posture: Produced by replay, restore, and privacy workflows; consumed by audit queries and release gates.
rows.label: Replay attestations, restore drill evidence, and erasure proofs
rows.rationale: These artifacts prove replay, restore, and erasure outcomes and therefore belong in append-only evidence storage rather than mutable control rows.
rows.retention_posture: Proof-bearing evidence with retention-limited explainability semantics where needed.

rows.backup_posture: Tier 0 or Tier 1 depending attachment criticality; metadata is authoritative even when blob bodies live elsewhere.
rows.eventing_posture: Scan transitions, publication commands, and read-side updates derive from this metadata plus object-store refs.
rows.label: Upload session and attachment metadata
rows.rationale: The product must preserve request binding, lane identity, and publish-time state durably even though the uploaded bytes live in object storage.
rows.retention_posture: Governed by request-binding, scan, publication, and replacement lifecycle rules.

rows.backup_posture: Inventory-linked backup or replication required for authoritative or non-regenerable blob bodies.
rows.eventing_posture: Blob create/scan/publish notifications are transport or projection concerns; the bodies themselves are immutable objects.
rows.label: Immutable upload, quarantine, and authority body blobs
rows.rationale: The corpus explicitly places payload, evidence, and artifact bodies in object storage referenced by immutable refs rather than inside transactional rows or queues.
rows.retention_posture: Blob bodies may age into tombstones or masked placeholders, but durable refs remain explicit.

rows.backup_posture: Direct backup for filed or authority-linked artifacts; regenerable exports may rebuild from authoritative refs if policy allows.
rows.eventing_posture: Rendered artifacts may trigger notifications or downloads, but the bodies remain immutable objects referenced from truth stores.
rows.label: Generated export and proof-bundle bodies
rows.rationale: Proof and export bodies are too large and too versioned for the primary control store, but still need immutable refs and retention-aware lifecycle handling.
rows.retention_posture: Retention-limited explainability rules determine whether bundles remain filing-capable, review-only, or tombstoned.

rows.eventing_posture: Refreshed by projectors from durable truth and then fanned out to clients.
rows.label: Derived read models and snapshots
rows.rationale: The deployment contract explicitly marks route-facing bundles and workspaces as derived disposable projections rather than command-side truth.
rows.retention_posture: Retention-limited projections must degrade explicitly and never overwrite evidence.

rows.backup_posture: Best-effort only; loss forces rebase or fresh snapshot issuance, not legal-state loss.
rows.eventing_posture: Feeds reconnect and stale-view handling; never authorizes legal mutation on its own.
rows.label: Resume tokens, route cursors, and server-side reconnect helpers
rows.rationale: Cursors and resume tokens are durable enough for reconnect ergonomics but explicitly not authoritative truth.
rows.retention_posture: Short-lived resumability aids with explicit invalidation and expiry semantics.

rows.backup_posture: No primary backup dependency; transport loss is acceptable when durable truth survives.
rows.eventing_posture: Primary role is delivery; legal mutation and history remain anchored elsewhere.
rows.label: Queue messages, broker envelopes, and worker claims
rows.rationale: The queue or broker is explicitly transport-only and must be rebuildable from durable outbox, inbox, receipt, interaction, workflow, and audit truth.

rows.backup_posture: No direct backup required; clients rebase to snapshots when history compacts or fanout is lost.
rows.eventing_posture: Idempotent-by-sequence client delivery with explicit rebase on compacted history or epoch change.
rows.label: Live SSE frames and notification fanout
rows.rationale: The stream contracts make live events reconnect-safe and typed, but still derived from snapshots and durable refs rather than canonical truth.
rows.retention_posture: Ephemeral fanout only; durable snapshots live elsewhere.

rows.backup_posture: No backup dependence; invalidate on revocation, masking change, tenant switch, or schema incompatibility.
rows.eventing_posture: Hydrated from authoritative snapshots and invalidated by session or schema events.
rows.label: Client-side disposable scene and route caches
rows.rationale: The browser and native session contracts permit local persistence only for derivable state and explicitly forbid it from becoming legal truth.

store_catalog.durability_posture: Direct backup required; Tier 0 for control-plane legal truth.
store_catalog.label: Primary control store
store_catalog.rebuildability: Not rebuildable from projections or transport.
store_catalog.role: Transactional system of record for mutable legal, workflow, control-plane, and idempotency state.

store_catalog.durability_posture: Direct backup required; Tier 0 for compliance-significant evidence.
store_catalog.label: Append-only audit store
store_catalog.rebuildability: Not rebuildable from projections; only derivable from preserved evidence itself.
store_catalog.role: Immutable or append-only evidence ledger for audit events, provenance, envelopes, ingress checkpoints, replay attestations, and erasure proofs.

store_catalog.durability_posture: Direct backup or inventory-linked replication required for non-regenerable bodies.
store_catalog.label: Immutable object store
store_catalog.rebuildability: Some generated exports can be regenerated; authoritative uploaded or authority-returned bodies cannot.
store_catalog.role: Blob storage for uploaded bytes, quarantined binaries, exports, proof bodies, response bodies, and other large immutable payloads referenced by durable refs.

store_catalog.label: Projection store
store_catalog.rebuildability: Rebuild from control truth, append-only evidence, and immutable object refs.
store_catalog.role: Disposable read-side materializations for route-facing bundles, snapshots, workspace views, and search/index helpers.

store_catalog.durability_posture: Transport loss tolerated if outbox/inbox truth survives.
store_catalog.label: Queue or broker
store_catalog.rebuildability: Rebuildable from durable outbox, inbox, receipt, interaction, workflow, and audit truth.
store_catalog.role: Transport-only delivery fabric for worker coordination, outbox drain, reconciliation jobs, notifications, and live fanout.

store_catalog.label: Cache and resume store
store_catalog.rebuildability: Rehydratable from authoritative snapshots, receipts, and projection state.
store_catalog.role: Ephemeral resumability, route cursor, session-adjacent cache, and reconnect helpers.
